extraction.one_line_summary: KEMNA Leonard won from 7-rider breakaway while Jumbo-Visma created echelons in opening hour at 32mph, splitting peloton before final climb
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma deployed seven riders into first echelon split, using full squad depth to pressure GC rivals early
UAE/Soudal drove gutter echelons aggressively rather than fanning out, prioritizing splits over peloton cohesion despite teammates behind
Time splits taken at 2.6km due to mudslide confusion, creating GC uncertainty between Almeida/Vlasov vs Roglic/Remco/Vingegaard group
Hamilton bridged solo to established breakaway with 3min advantage already built, signaling poor form entering finale

rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: caught behind initial echelon split, described as 'not where you want to be' minutes back
rider_signals.SEPKUSS Primoz: neutral: survived gutter echelons in third wheel with Remco/Vingegaard, but distanced on final 2km steep pitches losing seconds
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: positive: came through first of GC group at finish with Vlasov, gaining 5-7 seconds on Roglic/Remco/Vingegaard
rider_signals.HAMILTON Chris: negative: required massive solo effort to rejoin breakaway after initial establishment, unlikely to contend after energy expenditure
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: finished several seconds behind Almeida despite having teammate Kataneo for support in chase group
